meta.description: Quality assurance validation of all generated assets

step.name: run_asset_inventory
step.action: Run the T4 asset inventory task to catalog all generated assets in the episode directory. Compare the inventory against the SSD to identify: present assets (exist and match expected specs), missing assets (expected but not found), and extra assets (found but not in SSD). Build a comprehensive asset status matrix.
step.output: Asset inventory matrix with status per asset

step.name: validate_video_clips
step.action: For each video clip asset: verify file exists and is not corrupted (can be opened), check duration matches SSD specification (within 0.5s tolerance), check resolution matches SSD specification, verify codec is correct (H.264/H.265), check frame rate matches target, verify file size is reasonable (not suspiciously small or excessively large), and spot-check for visual quality (no extreme artifacts, color banding, or frozen frames).
step.output: Video clip validation report per clip

step.name: validate_narration_audio
step.action: For each narration audio file: verify file exists and plays correctly, check audio quality (no clipping, distortion, or background noise), verify speaking pace is appropriate, check duration aligns with scene timing, verify the voice matches the expected character configuration, and confirm text-to-speech accuracy (narration matches the script text).

step.name: validate_character_refs
step.action: For each character's reference images: verify all four angles exist (front, side, back, three-quarter), verify composite sheet exists, check visual consistency across angles (face, clothing, proportions), verify resolution meets the reference image standard, and check for AI artifacts (extra limbs, distorted features, inconsistent details).
step.output: Character reference validation report per character

step.name: validate_frames
step.action: For each first/last frame: verify file exists at the expected path, check resolution matches the target clip resolution, check aspect ratio matches the target clip, verify content matches the scene context, and check for AI artifacts. For extracted frames (from ffmpeg), verify extraction was clean with no encoding artifacts.

step.name: cross_check_against_ssd
step.action: Perform a comprehensive cross-check: every scene in the SSD has all required assets, every asset has the correct specifications per the SSD, scene-to-asset mappings are correct, multi-clip merge files exist for multi-clip scenes, and audio assets (music, SFX) are present and mapped.

step.name: check_merged_scene_continuity
step.action: For multi-clip merged scenes: play the merged video to check for visual continuity at clip boundaries, verify no jump cuts or jarring transitions between clips, check that audio timing aligns across clip boundaries, and verify the total merged duration matches the SSD scene duration.

step.name: generate_qa_report
step.action: Generate the comprehensive QA report using the asset-qa-checklist.md template. Include: overall pass/fail status, per-asset validation results, issues found (critical, warning, info), recommendations for assets needing regeneration, cost estimate for regeneration if needed, and sign-off readiness assessment. Gate: if all essential checks pass, the episode proceeds to assembly. If critical issues exist, flag specific assets for regeneration.
step.output: Complete QA report with gate decision

quality-gates.gate: All assets specified in the SSD are present in the episode directory
Video clip quality is acceptable (correct duration, resolution, codec, no major artifacts)
Audio is clean with no clipping, distortion, or background noise
Character visual consistency is maintained across all reference images
Multi-clip merged scenes have smooth continuity at boundaries
SSD cross-check passes with all scene-to-asset mappings correct
QA report is generated with clear pass/fail per asset
All critical issues are documented with regeneration recommendations
